title: Flow of a printf() System Call in UNIX
description: This flowchart illustrates the sequence of steps and key components involved when a user program executes the printf() function, showing its journey from user space to the kernel and hardware.
relationships: User Program calls printf() in libc
printf() formats string and calls write() syscall
write() syscall triggers a switch to kernel mode
Kernel processes the request and calls the appropriate device driver
Device driver writes data to the terminal buffer
